items.title: Housing price downturn accelerates as 'perfect storm' hits market

children.content: Australian home values record their biggest decline in more than three years as the housing market slowdown that began in Sydney and Melbourne spreads across the country.

items.title: 'Alarming': PM questions why Ernst and Young grads had access to banking data

children.content: Anthony Albanese has questioned how two Ernst and Young graduates had access to personal banking data after one was charged with accessing his account. Follow live.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese

items.title: Liberals can't keep on track even with a prize in sight

children.content: The Liberals, it seems, can't resist returning to damaging infighting even in the one part of the country where things have been going quite encouragingly for them.

image.altText: Moira Deeming appears serious as she walks through the parliament carpark, wearing a purple blazer.

items.title: Photos show room where police allege Thai teenager was last seen

children.content: New photographs reveal the cluttered room where 17-year-old Tunchanok Donhomla was allegedly murdered by Australian man Simon Peter Carman.

image.altText: A woman speaks to a man in a room.

items.title: ACCC knocks back Coles supermarket as watchdog flexes new powers

children.content: In an unprecedented move, Australia's consumer watchdog has exercised new powers to block the opening of a Coles supermarket, on the grounds it could knock out smaller competitors in the surrounding region.

image.altText: Shopping trolleys in a parking lot for a supermarket.

France players converge to celebrate a Bradley Barcola goal
Erling Haaland wearing a novelty viking hat

articles.title: French unity on display as their ruthless run through World Cup continues

children.content: In a French team littered with superstars it is something intangible that could stop them taking another World Cup title, following another clinical display against Sweden in the round of 32.

image.altText: Kylian Mbappe holds his arms in the air in celebration

articles.title: Socceroos coy on penalty preparations for World Cup knockout game

children.content: The Socceroos know their knockout game against Egypt could come down to penalties, but they aren't revealing anything about their strategies.

image.altText: Ajdin Hrustic

children.content: Kylian Mbappé scores twice for France as it tears Sweden apart, while Erling Haaland nets an 88th minute winner to send Norway through and eliminate Côte d'Ivoire. Follow live.

items.title: Wild weather in Mexico delays game, France's fantastic four dismantle Sweden

updates.title: Kick-off delayed for Mexico vs Ecuador
FT: France demolish Sweden 3-0 in New Jersey!

items.title: Vanuatu deal is significant but a harbinger of what's to come in the Pacific

children.content: Although a big moment in diplomacy, the Vanuatu agreement has come at a clear reputational cost to Australia.

image.altText: Jotham Napat and Anthony Albanese hold up signed documents.

items.title: Harmful conspiracy sustained anti-Jewish hate online, commission hears

children.content: The royal commission has heard new research which found anti-Jewish hate increased online both after October 7, 2023 and the Bondi Beach terror attack in December.

image.altText: A man's hands typing at a laptop in a dark room.

items.title: Maya Joint's 'craziest moment' and a pleasant surprise on centre court

children.content: Australian Maya Joint had to catch a glimpse of Serena Williams in the days leading up to their Wimbledon opener just to remove some of the mythos around the 23-time grand slam champion.

articles.title: Who is Maya Joint, Australian conqueror of Serena at Wimbledon?

children.content: Maya Joint was born in April 2006 when Serena Williams had already won seven Grand Slam singles titles. So who is the 20-year-old who just defeated a tennis legend in her return to the All England Club?

image.altText: A red-headed female tennis player in white pokes tongue out on Wimbledon centre court

articles.title: Australian rising star takes down Serena Williams in Wimbledon return

children.content: Australian Maya Joint holds her nerve to beat tennis legend Serena Williams in the 23-time grand slam champion's first professional tennis match in four years.

image.altText: Maya Joint shakes Serena Williams's hand over the net at Wimbledon.

items.title: Mother of murdered Toyah Cordingley gives rare interview to thank police officers

children.content: Eight years on from her daughter's brutal murder, Vanessa Gardiner says nominating the investigating offices for an award was one of the few ways she could express her gratitude.

image.altText: Woman wearing head scarf beside woman with glasses with her hand on her shoulder

items.title: Childcare provider stripped of funding labels decision 'devastating'

children.content: A Victorian childcare provider stripped of its federal government funding over unresolved safety breaches has vowed to challenge the decision.

image.altText: A non-identifiable child playing with toys.

items.title: Victims of alleged sexual abuse question mental therapy's legal status

children.content: Victim-survivors of alleged sexual abuse have spoken to the ABC of the tension between EMDR mental health therapy and seeking justice through the legal system.

gallery.altText: Tess Hassell stands in front of metal fencing as she looks at the camera

thumbnail.altText: Tess Hassell stands in front of metal fencing as she looks at the camera

items.title: ASX dips at open, while AI-buying frenzy drives Dow to record high

children.content: The Australian share market eases slightly in early trade, even as an AI stock-led rally boosted the Dow Jones to a record high for a second straight day. Follow live.

image.altText: ASX logo and board

items.title: Canberra man pleads guilty to performing Nazi salutes in public

children.content: A Canberra man has pleaded guilty after performing Nazi salutes at two suburban shopping centres and placing white supremacist stickers over Indigenous and multicultural signs at the Australian National University.

image.altText: A young man leaves court.

items.title: One of the best movies of 2026 cost less than $1 million

items.title: The condition that causes people to get lost in their own home

children.content: Some estimates suggest up to one in 30 people may be affected by a condition that has been described as a lifelong inability to orient oneself — even in extremely familiar surroundings.

image.altText: A man stands in a stairwell

items.title: Coalition avoids direct One Nation messaging in daily talking points

children.content: Coalition talking points, obtained by the ABC, make almost no direct mention of One Nation despite the minor party surging in the polls.

image.altText: A man in a suit takes questions from journalists

items.title: Alcoa to buy South32's WA bauxite mine in $8b deal

children.content: American mining giant Alcoa is set to buy $8 billion worth of assets from Australia's South32.

image.altText: Massive industrial crusher surrounded by strip mined land and red dirt

items.title: Scientists prepare natural enemy to fight one of Australia's worst weeds

children.content: An imported plant pathogen that will spread on the wind is being readied for release against one of Australia's worst weeds.

gallery.altText: A man in laboratory gear holds up a damaged lantana leaf with more plants behind him.

thumbnail.altText: A man in laboratory gear holds up a damaged lantana leaf with more plants behind him.

items.title: Certain sports have a high risk of brain injury. What can you do as a parent?

children.content: When your child decides they are keen on playing sport, there are lots of factors to consider as a parent, and after recent news stories about chronic traumatic encephalopathy, keeping them safe from head injuries may be front of mind.

image.altText: A group of teens wearing team soccer jerseys celebrate on the field.

items.title: Woman raises awareness of farmers' mental health after husband's death

children.content: Leanne Staniforth says her husband, John, suffered in silence before his death by suicide. She is raising her voice to break down the stigma around depression.

image.altText: A woman looks at the camera with a sombre expression holding a book

items.title: Showcase of electric mine trucks signals long road still ahead

children.content: BHP and Rio Tinto have put their two battery-electric trucks on show, but is an aversion to risk stifling meaningful change?

gallery.altText: A huge, yellow mining dump truck on a red dirt mine site.

thumbnail.altText: A huge, yellow mining dump truck on a red dirt mine site.

items.title: 'Fear and panic' as more NDIS registration rules loom

children.content: Uncertainty and confusion are sweeping across the NDIS provider sector with mandatory registration for two categories of providers starting today.

image.altText: A young adult male standing in a park and looking at a camera

items.title: 'Recurring issues' uncovered following surveillance of chopper sector

children.content: Australia's aviation watchdog has warned that cutting corners on maintenance and certification requirements could create a "real safety risk".

image.altText: A chopper shot from below, it is parked on green grass and a blue sky can be seen in the background.

items.title: When working more hours might mean you lose your home

children.content: Fremantle resident Trish Owen could lose the tenancy on her government-provided home if she works more hours — and a new study suggests many people are in the same situation.

items.title: 'Not fair and honest': Accounting firms face split after KPMG scandal

children.content: Accounting firms could be asked to split their lucrative consulting services from their audit functions and individual firm partners could face far greater scrutiny and penalties for legal breaches, under a shake up of the industry being consulted on by the federal government.

image.altText: People walk past a KPMG sign

items.title: Renewed push to ban 'conversion practices' in Tasmania

children.content: Tasmania was the last Australian jurisdiction to decriminalise homosexuality, but since then it has gone on to pass some of the country's most inclusive legislation. There's one final frontier — conversion practices.

image.altText: A large rainbow flag is waved in the air.

items.title: Venezuelan earthquake rescuers save 3yo boy after six days under rubble

children.content: The death toll from Venezuela's devastating two earthquakes has risen to more than 1,900 as tens of thousands of people still remain unaccounted for.

items.title: Volunteers say National Trust of SA warned them against speaking publicly

children.content: Volunteers who spoke to the media say they felt "threatened" with formal warnings from the National Trust of South Australia. They are now preparing to share their experiences in a parliamentary inquiry.

items.title: De Minaur one of only two Aussie men through to Wimbledon second round

children.content: Alex de Minaur makes a strong start to Wimbledon in a first round that saw carnage for his Australian counterparts.

image.altText: Alex de Minaur hits a forehand at Wimbledon.

items.title: US Supreme Court rejects Trump's bid to end birthright citizenship

children.content: Donald Trump's controversial attempt to restrict birthright citizenship in the US is rejected by the Supreme Court, up-ending the president's hardline agenda on immigration.
